vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current biological state. P1 (Yellow Pot) and P2/P4 (Black Pot) are devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit) contains a single, solitary sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Partial Survival: Single sprout present; Pothos/Rabbit anchor confirmed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface. No signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Single green sprout emerging from soil near the rabbit anchor. Appears to be a singular Pothos shoot.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface. No signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, moist, and appears to be high in organic matter. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The desk surface shows debris/white particulate matter near the pots. The pots appear to be largely dormant or failed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: No significant change; pots remain empty.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Emergence of a single green sprout in P3.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The sprout in P3 has elongated slightly, indicating active growth.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total collapse. Only one specimen (P3) shows signs of life. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively dead/lost as no vegetative structures are visible.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white debris on the soil surface in P3 and the surrounding desk area suggests potential mineral buildup or potting medium degradation.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided sequence. The initial state shows a barren environment. Over the course of the observation, a single sprout emerged in the P3 pot. The rest of the registry (P1, P2, P4) shows no signs of life, confirming a systemic loss of the expected botanical specimens. The environment is currently supporting only one minor sprout.
